name: $ Cash Advance
description_short: ACE Cash Express offers payday loans ($100–$255) and short-term cash advances in Hawthorne, CA, with online and in-store application options for quick funding.
description_long:
ACE Cash Express is an established financial services company operating physical retail locations across the United States, including a Hawthorne, California store at 3928 W Rosecrans Ave. The company specializes in small-dollar, short-term lending products designed to bridge gaps between paychecks for consumers facing unexpected expenses. ACE's core payday loan product provides loans ranging from $100 to $255, available both online and at their physical locations, with repayment expected in full on the borrower's next payday.

Beyond payday loans, ACE Cash Express operates a comprehensive financial services platform. They offer check cashing without requiring a bank account (only a valid government-issued ID), bill payment services for rent and utilities, MoneyGram money transfers to over 200 countries, money orders, ATM services, and tax preparation services. The company also provides prepaid card products (ACE Elite Visa Card, Flare Account, and Porte mobile banking account) in partnership with Pathward, N.A., a federally chartered bank and FDIC member.

ACE Cash Express differentiates itself through its hybrid online-and-brick-and-mortar model, allowing customers to apply for payday loans either digitally or in person. The company maintains convenient store hours (9 AM–7 PM weekdays, 9 AM–5 PM Saturdays) and provides in-person support for account management and cash transactions. Their integration with Pathward for banking products and MoneyGram for money transfers positions them as a comprehensive alternative financial services provider rather than a single-product lender.

However, payday loans carry significant financial risks. While the website does not explicitly disclose APR or fee structures, California payday loans are subject to state caps on fees, but borrowers can still incur substantial costs. The short repayment term (full balance due on next payday) creates a cycle risk for consumers who cannot repay in full, potentially leading to repeated borrowing. This product is best suited as a genuine emergency measure, not a regular financial strategy.
pros: Low minimum loan amount ($100) accessible to consumers with minimal credit
Multiple application channels (online and in-store) for convenience
Same-day or next-day funding available for approved applicants
No bank account required for check cashing (valid ID only)
Extended store hours (open until 7 PM weekdays, 5 PM Saturdays)
Comprehensive financial services ecosystem including money transfers, bill pay, and prepaid cards
Physical retail locations provide in-person support and account management
cons: APR and fee structure not disclosed on website; payday loans typically carry triple-digit annual rates even within California's regulatory limits
Full repayment due on next payday creates cash flow risk for consumers living paycheck-to-paycheck
Short-term loan structure can lead to debt cycles if borrowers cannot repay in full and roll over the loan
Limited loan amounts ($100–$255 maximum) may not address larger emergency expenses
No mention of credit-building or alternative products for consumers seeking to improve financial standing
best_for: Consumers with immediate, small emergency expenses ($100–$255) and predictable next paycheck income
Unbanked or underbanked individuals who need check cashing or bill payment without a bank account
People in urgent need of domestic or international money transfers via MoneyGram
diagnosis: ACE Cash Express is best for unbanked consumers or those facing small, immediate cash emergencies who have predictable paycheck income and can repay within 2–4 weeks. The main caveat is that payday loans, regardless of regulatory environment, carry high effective costs and should only be used as a genuine emergency measure—not as a regular financial product—to avoid debt cycles.
services: ACE Elite Visa prepaid debit card
Porte mobile banking app with in-person support
